children.name: Galbula
children.description: Slender, long-billed jacamars with brilliant metallic plumage in shades of green, bronze, and rufous. They perch upright on exposed branches in a characteristic kingfisher-like posture, sallying out to catch butterflies, dragonflies, and other flying insects with an audible snap of the bill. They are among the most beautiful birds of the Neotropics.

children.name: Galbula ruficauda
children.description: The most widespread and familiar jacamar, ranging from southern Mexico through Central America and across much of South America east of the Andes. It has a brilliant green head and back, a rufous tail and belly, and a white throat. It is commonly found along forest edges and streams, perched low on exposed twigs.

children.name: Galbula galbula
children.description: Found in the Amazon basin of northern Brazil, the Guianas, and southern Venezuela. As its name suggests, it has a green tail unlike the rufous tail of the similar Rufous-tailed Jacamar. It frequents humid lowland forests and gallery forests along rivers.

children.name: Galbula leucogastra
children.description: A distinctive jacamar of the Amazon and Orinoco basins, with bronzy-green upperparts and a white belly and undertail. The male has a brilliant iridescent coppery-bronze sheen on the upperparts, while the female is duller. It favours sandy-soil forests and terra firme rainforest.

children.name: Galbula dea
children.description: A strikingly long-tailed jacamar of the Amazon and Guiana Shield regions. It has dark green upperparts, a white throat, and an exceptionally long, graduated tail. It is named for its elegant, almost ethereal appearance as it perches motionless in the rainforest mid-storey.

children.name: Galbula cyanescens
children.description: A jacamar with bluish front from the southern Amazon.

children.name: Galbula pastazae
children.description: A jacamar with coppery chest from the eastern Andes.

children.name: Galbula tombacea
children.description: A jacamar with white chin from the western Amazon.

children.name: Galbula chalcothorax
children.description: A purplish jacamar from the southern and western Amazon.

children.name: Brachygalba
children.description: Small, dull-plumaged jacamars found in the forests of South America.

children.name: Brachygalba salmoni
children.description: A jacamar from the Choco region of Colombia and Panama.

children.name: Brachygalba lugubris
children.description: A brown jacamar found in the Amazon and Orinoco basins.

children.name: Brachygalba goeringi
children.description: A jacamar with pale head from Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Brachygalba albogularis
children.description: A jacamar with white throat found in Peru and Bolivia.

children.name: Jacamerops
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the largest of all jacamars.

children.name: Jacamerops aureus
children.description: The largest jacamar, with iridescent green plumage and massive bill, found from Costa Rica to the Amazon.

children.name: Galbula
children.description: A genus with two species from Central and South America.

children.name: Galbula purusiana
children.description: A jacamar found in the Purus River region of Brazil.
